title: Latency benchmark is enforced as a deterministic unit-test assertion
claim: The equivalence suite asserts bfsMs <= cteMs from a 25-iteration in-process mean, so ordinary scheduler or cache noise can fail the release gate even when behavior is correct.
counterevidenceSought: Checked the benchmark helper and test body for warmup, percentile sampling, tolerance, or advisory-only recording mode; none was present.
alternativeExplanation: A local run recorded BFS faster than CTE, but a single local mean does not make the assertion stable across CI hosts and concurrent load.
downgradeTrigger: Downgrade to P2 after the latency comparison is moved to advisory reporting or hardened with warmup, percentile sampling, and a documented tolerance.
